page: Microsoft Azure M12ds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MDSMediumMemoryv3 family. Built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by Intel processors, this instance features 12 dedicated vCPUs and a substantial memory allocation of 240.0 GB, yielding 20.0 GB of RAM per core. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration includes 410 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk attachments. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or integrated GPU hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the M12ds_v3 offers high memory density relative to its compute core count, making it a specialized choice for workloads that require large memory footprints without needing massive parallel CPU processing. It is suited for memory-intensive applications, including medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, and enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure M12ds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MDSMediumMemoryv3 family, designed for workloads requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance provides 12 dedicated vCPUs and 240.0 GB of RAM, translating to 20.0 GB of memory per core. The server features 410 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage for demanding disk I/O requirements. It does not include integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The high memory density of the M12ds_v3 makes it qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound workloads that do not require excessive compute scaling. This instance is suited for hosting medium-sized databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications.
